title: Software Update - DFU - HS low battery: multiple clicks on buttons
scenario: DFU is allowed despite headset battery below 5%, and rapid button clicks break dialog flow on low battery
steps: 1. Launch Poly Studio Mobile app on Android.
2. Navigate to Home screen and connect a Poly Bluetooth headset.
3. Ensure the headset battery level is below 5% and the headset is NOT plugged into power.
4. Go to Device Settings → [Device Name] → Firmware Update (or DFU).
5. Observe the UI: verify that the update/continue/start button is disabled or a warning is shown indicating insufficient battery.
6. Attempt to rapidly click (multiple times) on the update/continue/start button.
7. If a dialog appears (e.g., 'Battery too low to update'), rapidly click the OK / Confirm button multiple times.
8. Observe the app behavior and dialog flow after the rapid clicks.
expected: The DFU process should not start. The app should display a clear warning that the headset battery is too low (below 5%) and block the update. Rapid clicking on buttons should not bypass this check, and the app should remain stable without crashing or entering an unexpected state.
